facts.text: Paris hosted the Summer Olympics three times, in 1900, 1924, and 2024.
The nickname "City of Light" stems from Paris's role in the Age of Enlightenment and its early adoption of street lighting.
Paris was originally the Roman town Lutetia Parisiorum, later called Lutèce, before becoming the capital of France.
The Hôtel-Dieu, founded in 651, is the oldest hospital still operating in Paris and worldwide, though its current building dates to 1877.
Paris's museums include the Musée d'Orsay, Musée Marmottan Monet, and Musée de l'Orangerie for Impressionist art, plus Musée Rodin and Musée Picasso for modern works.
